California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 13, 1987


J. Dennis Crabb
City Attorney
City Of South Lake Tahoe
P.O. Box 1210
South Lake Tahoe, CA  95705

                                                Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
                                                Our Advice No. I-87-035

Dear Mr. Crabb:

     Pursuant to our telephone discussion, you have forwarded to
me the attached copy of your legal memorandum to South Lake
Tahoe Mayor Woods. I have reviewed your memorandum and agree
with the advice and its conclusion as it relates to the conflict
of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

     I trust this letter is sufficient confirmation for your
purposes.  Should you have further questions regarding this
matter, I may be reached at 916/322-5901.

                                                Very truly yours,

                                                Diane M. Griffiths
                                                General Counsel

                                                By: Robert E. Leigh
                                                Counsel, Legal Division

REL;km
Enclosures

______________

     1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative
Code Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are
to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.

     Your letter states only a general question; it does not seek
advice concerning a specific decision pending before the City
Council. Therefore, we consider it to be a request for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed).
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

.

CITY OF

SOUTH LAKE TAHOE
CALIFORNIA

JAN 28 8 41 AM '07

ADMINISTRATIVE CENTER
1052 TATA LANE
P.O. BOX 1210 — SOUTH LAKE TAHOE, CALIFORNIA 95705 — PHONE (916) 573-2040

TO:

Mayor Woods

FROM:

J. Dennis Crabb, City Attorney

DATE:

January 27, 1987

SUBJECT:

Processing of Permit Application

In accordance with the direction of the City Council on January
20, 1987, this memo is intended to constitute a formal City
Attorney's opinion as to the mechanisms necessary to the
correct processing of a permit application for the development
of real property owned by you and your wife on Eloise Avenue.

In processing the proposed development application the
following procedures must be observed:

1.

You have previously retained an independent consultant to
handle the processing of the application through both the
City and TRPA. All contact with the City staff must be
handled exclusively through the consultant. You should have
no contact of any type concerning the application with any
member of the City staff other than to request any other
legal advice which may be required from this office through
the City Council under the procedure established for that
purpose.

2.

All contacts between the consultant and the City staff must
be fully documented, in writing, and made a part of the
official City file regarding the project.

3.

The Planning Director will designate a single contact per-
son for the project on the City staff. All communications
between the City and the consultant must be handled by that
single individual. Until further notice, the designated
individual is Gary Marchio.

4.

It is not anticipated that this matter will come before the
City Council. Should that eventually occur, upon appeal or
otherwise, you must disqualify yourself from any such pro-

Mayor Woods
January 27, 1987
Page 2


ceeding and you must refrain from discussing the matter with
any member of the Council.

5. You should secure a written opinion from the counsel for
the Tahoe Regional Planning Agency regarding processing of
any application before that agency and insure that a copy
of that opinion is included in both the TRPA and City pro-
ject files.

Prior to preparing this memorandum, I consulted with Mr. Bob
Leidigh of the Fair Political Practices Commission and he
generally concurred with the advice provided. He did suggest
that I contact Mr. Ted Primm of the Attorney General's Office
for advice on whether a Government Code 1090 situation might be
involved.

That section prohibits entry into contracts with the City by
persons who are city officials, with certain limited excep-
tions. I do not believe that it has any applicability here.
However, Mr. Primm indicated that he wished to discuss it
further within his office and he will subsequently advise me if
I appear to be in error.

By copy of this memo I am advising the FPPC, Attorney General,
mPPA, and City staff of the advice provided to you and the
manner in which, in my opinion, the application should be pro-
cessed.

If they have further comments, I will advise you in writing.
In the interim, City staff is working with the consultant to
insure that the application to be submitted to the City is
complete. The consultant has executed a waiver of the statu-
tory time limits within which an application must be processed,
once it is determined to be complete. The Planning Department
will be working jointly with the TRPA staff to insure that
their requirements and any requirements under the presently
existing injunction are met during processing.

I would be less than candid if I did not state that the advice
I have provided to you is very conservative and there are, in
all probability, other lawful mechanisms which could be uti-

Mayor Woods
January 27, 1987
Page 3


lized. However, given the complex legal and governmental
questions involved with the new TRPA plan, its limits on new
development, and your role as a member of both the City Council
and the TRPA, I would be professionally remiss if I did not
advise a course of action which is circumspect in the extreme.

If there are any questions or you require clarification of any
issues discussed herein, please contact me at your convenience.


J. Dennis Crabb, City Attorney

JDC:jmc

cc:  City Manager
     City Clerk
     Planning Director
     ✓Bob Leidigh, Fair Political Practices Commission
     Ted Primm, Office of the Attorney General
     Susan Scholley, Legal Counsel, TRPA
     Dena Schwarte

Ivan C. Gayler, David J. Winkler, Principals

A-86-035

THE DEL MAR PARTNERSHIP
300 Fifteenth Street, Del Mar, California 92014 • 619-481-0903
JAN 20 1987

January 22, 1987


Diane M. Griffiths, General Counsel
Jeanne Pritchard, Division Chief
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 94804-0807

RE: Your File No. A-87-023

Dear Ms. Griffiths and Ms. Pritchard:

Thank you for your letter of January 16, 1987 regarding our duties under the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.

Please note that my partner Ivan C. Gayler and I transferred title of the subject properties to Del Mar Plaza, Ltd., a California limited partnership. That entity is filing Form 461 as an Independent Expenditure and Major Donor Committee.

Pursuant to your letter, the Partnership has prepared one statement for the period from January 1, 1986 through December 20, 1986. This statement is being filed late because we did not receive your letter until January 16, 1987 which indicated the filing requirements which had to be met. We did not realize until late December 1986 that we would have to report staff time, architectural fees, office overhead and other expenses associated with the preparation of ballot materials and payments for the costs of conducting the election.

The second statement is for the period from December 21, 1986 through January 17, 1987. It reflects the percentage of staff time allocated to expenses associated with informing voters about the facts related to the project. Salaried individual staff members who devoted more than ten percent (10%) of their working time to the election are reported. According to your explanation these are all expenditures "made for political purposes," which are therefore fully reportable.

If there is any additional information or clarification required please do not hesitate to call.

Yours truly,

David J. Winkler

California

Practices Commission


January 29, 1987


Dennis J. Crabb
City Attorney
P.O. Box 1210
South Lake Tahoe, CA  95705

Re:  87-035

Dear Mr. Crabb:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on January 28, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Robert E. Leidigh, an attorney
in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days.  You also should be aware that your
letter and our response are public records which may be
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: s involved with the new TRPA plan, its limits on new 
development, and your role as a member of both the City Council 
and the TRPA, I would be professionally remiss if I did not 
advise a course of action which is circumspect in the extreme. 
If there are any questions or you require clarification of any 
issues discussed herein, please contact me at your convenience. 
J. Dennis Crabb, City Attorney

City Clerk 
~lanning Director 
/Bob Leidigh, -0807 
RE: 
Your Dear Ms. Griffiths and Ms. Pritchard: 
Thank you for your letter of January 16, 1987 regarding our duties under the 
campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
Please note that my partner Ivan C. Gayler and I transferred title of the 
subject properties to Del Mar Plaza, Ltd., a California limited partnership. 
That entity is filing Form 461 as an Independent Expenditure and Major 
Donor Committee. 
Pursuant to your letter, the Partnership has prepared one statement for the 
period from January 1, 1986 through December 20, 1986. 
This statement is 
being filed late because we did not receive your letter until January 16, 
1987 which indicated the filing requirements which had to be met. 
We did 
not realize until late December 1986 that we would have to report staff 
twice, architectural fees, office overhead and other expenses associated with 
the preparation of ballot materials and payments for the costs of conducting 
the election. 
The second statement is for the period from December 21, 1986 through 
January 17, 1987. It reflects the percentage of staff time allocated to 
expenses associated with informing voters about the facts related to the 
project. Salaried individual staff members who devoted more than ten 
percent (10%) of their working time to the election are reported. 
According 
to your explanation these are all expenditures "made for political purposes," 
which are therefore fully reportable. 
If there is any additional information or clarification required please do not 
hesitate to call.

embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission February 13, 1987 J. Dennis Crabb City Attorney City Of South Lake Tahoe P.O. Box 1210 South Lake Tahoe, CA 95705 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our Advice No. I-87-035 Dear Mr. Crabb: Pursuant to our telephone discussion, you have forwarded to me the attached copy of your legal memorandum to South Lake Tahoe Mayor Woods. I have reviewed your memorandum and agree with the advice and its conclusion as it relates to the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ I trust this letter is sufficient confirmation for your purposes. Should you have further questions regarding this matter, I may be reached at 916/322-5901. Very truly yours, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel By: Robert E. Leigh Counsel, Legal Division REL;km Enclosures ______________ 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code. Your letter states only a general question; it does not seek advice concerning a specific decision pending before the City Council. Therefore, we consider it to be a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed). Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).) 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 . CITY OF SOUTH LAKE TAHOE CALIFORNIA JAN 28 8 41 AM '07 ADMINISTRATIVE CENTER 1052 TATA LANE P.O. BOX 1210 — SOUTH LAKE TAHOE, CALIFORNIA 95705 — PHONE (916) 573-2040 TO: Mayor Woods FROM: J. Dennis Crabb, City Attorney DATE: January 27, 1987 SUBJECT: Processing of Permit Application In accordance with the direction of the City Council on January 20, 1987, this memo is intended to constitute a formal City Attorney's opinion as to the mechanisms necessary to the correct processing of a permit application for the development of real property owned by you and your wife on Eloise Avenue. In processing the proposed development application the following procedures must be observed: 1. You have previously retained an independent consultant to handle the processing of the application through both the City and TRPA. All contact with the City staff must be handled exclusively through the consultant. You should have no contact of any type concerning the application with any member of the City staff other than to request any other legal advice which may be required from this office through the City Council under the procedure established for that purpose. 2. All contacts between the consultant and the City staff must be fully documented, in writing, and made a part of the official City file regarding the project. 3. The Planning Director will designate a single contact per- son for the project on the City staff. All communications between the City and the consultant must be handled by that single
